disclaimer: PROJECTION, not a forecast. The headline is the data-center-specific impact: infrastructure costs filed in IURC dockets, split across the utility's customers and amortized over 5 years. It is shown next to your current bill and this year's overall rate change for context. Real cost allocation is decided in rate cases and is contested. Update any figure in this file and every calculation updates automatically. See METHODOLOGY.md.

source.label: Indiana Capital Chronicle: the 'big five' defend rates

equation: current_bill = kwh × rate ; dc_impact_per_month = Σ(filed data-center infrastructure $) ÷ customers ÷ (amortize_years × 12) ; range = dc_impact × (1 ± uncertainty_band)

utilities.notes: Meta Jeffersonville is the largest single load in Duke's Indiana territory; upgrade costs are recovered from all customers.
AES claims the Google Monrovia deal (Cause 46394) nets $770M in customer savings over 15 years — a contested framing; Google is contracted to pay its own infrastructure.

cost_shifts.label: Google Fort Wayne + Amazon New Carlisle interconnection

utilities.notes: Serving Google Fort Wayne + Amazon New Carlisle. Also recovering uneconomic coal-contract costs through 2040 (Cause 45164 RA-5).

Amazon (Cause 46322, approved June 17 2026) and Alphabet/Google (Cause 46393, filed April 16 2026) are served under customer-specific contracts through NIPSCO Generation LLC, not from rate base — so their ~$7B of generation and transmission is not charged to ratepayers. NIPSCO projects the reverse: a residential credit of $7-9/month by 2032 via the Shared System Charge, about half of it guaranteed. Two open exposures kept out of this number because they are not quantified: the OUCC has appealed the ring-fencing order (Cause 46183) to the Indiana Court of Appeals, and NIPSCO's regulated 2024 IRP adds up to 3,835 MW of gas whose only load-growth driver, per OUCC comments, is data centers.

sources.label: NiSource — regulatory approvals, data center strategy (June 23, 2026)

utilities.notes: SW Indiana; second-highest rate after a 25% jump. A ~600 MW data center is proposed in the Gibson/Posey area in CenterPoint territory.
